name: TAO Post Expansion Runner v1
edge_mechanism: post-expansion partial-profit runner structure that keeps a core position for trend persistence
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether taking partial profits early while keeping a small structure-managed core can outperform one-shot exits after an expansion move. The edge is management asymmetry after a real expansion, not a rarer entry trigger.
invalidation_condition: Abort if expansion entries remain too sparse or if the runner portion adds churn without improving total expectancy after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price expands out of a local compression or reclaim, pays a first objective, and then either persists enough for a small runner or quickly mean-reverts.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: On volatile assets, expansion moves often deliver immediate distance but occasionally continue much farther. Partial-profit plus a core runner can capture both behaviors without needing a rarer entry condition.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best lane because expansion bursts are meaningful and runners can materially matter on this asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The first target may hit, but the runner can give back too much if follow-through rarely persists.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense enough but runner value is weak, reduce runner size or tighten structure exit before changing the entry logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A decent number of trades where early profit is common and only a minority develop into meaningful runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus one cross-lane show real density, early profit capture, and occasional profitable runner persistence after costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or runner logic that consistently gives back expectancy.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; expansion qualification and runner management use distinct roles
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using broad expansion-after-reclaim logic rather than rare breakout perfection

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for testing runner management after expansion on a volatile asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: Entries occur often enough and the runner adds value after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Expansions are too sparse or runner logic gives back too much.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent early profits with a smaller set of meaningful runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same management asymmetry works on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the early-profit plus runner pattern with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only works on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but less explosive runner behavior than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the management logic transfers to the other major in the basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows similar early-profit and occasional runner persistence.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More stable but smaller runner contribution than TAO.

long: Trail remaining 25% by EMA_20 with fallback 0.68 * ATR_14
short: Trail remaining 25% by EMA_20 with fallback 0.68 * ATR_14
